mnemonics: SUPER User Privileges Require Extreme Responsibility
keyPoints: The super user (root) has unrestricted access to all commands and files.
The 'su' command allows a user to switch to the super user account.
The 'sudo' command allows a permitted user to execute a command as the super user.
commonMistakes: Using the root account for routine tasks instead of a regular user account.
Forgetting to exit a root shell after completing a privileged task, leaving the session vulnerable.
